data.title: Effect of Mirabegron on Bladder Compliance ; a Prospective Paired Comparison of Mirabegron and Anticholinergics in Patients With Low Bladder Compliance
data.brief_summary: In many cases of neurogenic bladder patients, bladder compliance is lowered, which is a factor that deteriorates renal function. It is known that deterioration of renal function is accompanied in 50 to 70% of patients with low bladder compliance. For patients with poor bladder compliance, a consensus has not yet been established regarding the treatment method. This is especially true in the case of drug treatment rather than surgical methods. data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Patients over 19 years of age
2. Bladder compliance is 20ml/cmH2O or less
3. Patient who has been treated with antimuscarinic for at least 1 month, but has not been effective.
4. Patient who agrees to and signs the informed consent
